response:
youyou
to find equilibrium set derivative to zero so rP(1-P/K) equals h(t) with r as 0.1 and K as 1000 and h(t) as 10 plus 5sin(pi/6 t) this gives 0.1P(1-P/1000) = 10 + 5sin(pi/6 t) solving for P yields two values but only positive real roots matter for population stability analysis
for part two with K(t) = 1000 - 3t the equation becomes 0.1P(1-P/(1000-3t)) = 10 + 5sin(pi/6 t) the population becomes unsustainable when carrying capacity drops so low that no positive equilibrium exists or when P_e drops to zero which happens when K(t) approaches the minimum required for survival given the harvesting rate
the critical time occurs when 1000 - 3t equals zero or when the discriminant of the quadratic becomes negative but simplest check is when K(t) = 0 so t = 333.33 years yet with harvesting the collapse happens earlier when K(t) is too small to support even the minimum harvest
a palindrome is noon
no dots used anywhere in this text
